verses.text: 'The wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bold as a lion'; for a land's sin its rulers multiply, but a wise ruler prolongs its order; a poor man who oppresses the poor is like a sweeping rain leaving no food. Guilt makes men fearful. The righteous are bold; cruel rule ruins a land.

'The wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bold as a lion'; for a land's sin its rulers multiply, but a wise ruler prolongs its order; a poor man who oppresses the poor is like a sweeping rain leaving no food. Guilt makes men fearful. The righteous are bold; cruel rule ruins a land.

'The wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bold as a lion'; for a land's sin its rulers multiply, but a wise ruler prolongs its order; a poor man who oppresses the poor is like a sweeping rain leaving no food. Guilt makes men fearful. The righteous are bold; cruel rule ruins a land.

They that forsake the law praise the wicked, but they that keep it contend with them; evil men understand not judgment, but seekers of the LORD understand all; 'better is the poor that walketh in his uprightness' than the rich and perverse. Truth divides. Integrity outweighs crooked riches.

They that forsake the law praise the wicked, but they that keep it contend with them; evil men understand not judgment, but seekers of the LORD understand all; 'better is the poor that walketh in his uprightness' than the rich and perverse. Truth divides. Integrity outweighs crooked riches.

They that forsake the law praise the wicked, but they that keep it contend with them; evil men understand not judgment, but seekers of the LORD understand all; 'better is the poor that walketh in his uprightness' than the rich and perverse. Truth divides. Integrity outweighs crooked riches.

Whoso keeps the law is a wise son, but a companion of riotous men shames his father; gain by usury shall pass to one who pities the poor; 'he that turneth away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer shall be abomination.' Obedience is wisdom. Refusing God's word poisons even our prayers.

Whoso keeps the law is a wise son, but a companion of riotous men shames his father; gain by usury shall pass to one who pities the poor; 'he that turneth away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer shall be abomination.' Obedience is wisdom. Refusing God's word poisons even our prayers.

Whoso keeps the law is a wise son, but a companion of riotous men shames his father; gain by usury shall pass to one who pities the poor; 'he that turneth away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer shall be abomination.' Obedience is wisdom. Refusing God's word poisons even our prayers.

He that leads the upright astray falls into his own pit; the rich is wise in his own conceit, but the poor man of understanding sees through him; when the righteous rejoice, there is glory. Schemes recoil. The honest discern what the self-satisfied cannot.

'He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy'; happy is the man that fears always; a wicked ruler over the poor is a roaring lion. Confession brings mercy. Holy fear keeps the heart, but cruel power devours.

'He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy'; happy is the man that fears always; a wicked ruler over the poor is a roaring lion. Confession brings mercy. Holy fear keeps the heart, but cruel power devours.

'He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy'; happy is the man that fears always; a wicked ruler over the poor is a roaring lion. Confession brings mercy. Holy fear keeps the heart, but cruel power devours.

A prince void of understanding is a great oppressor, but he that hates covetousness prolongs his days; a man burdened with blood will flee to the pit; 'whoso walketh uprightly shall be saved.' Greed shortens rule. The upright walk is the safe one.

He that tills his land shall have plenty, but the idle dreamer shall have poverty; a faithful man abounds with blessings, but he that hastes to be rich shall not be innocent; respecting persons and chasing wealth lead to want. Diligence and integrity prosper. Greedy haste cannot stay honest.

He that tills his land shall have plenty, but the idle dreamer shall have poverty; a faithful man abounds with blessings, but he that hastes to be rich shall not be innocent; respecting persons and chasing wealth lead to want. Diligence and integrity prosper. Greedy haste cannot stay honest.

He that rebukes a man shall later find more favour than the flatterer; he that robs his parents and excuses it is a destroyer's companion; 'he that putteth his trust in the LORD shall be made fat.' Honest rebuke wins esteem. Trust in God brings true prosperity.

He that rebukes a man shall later find more favour than the flatterer; he that robs his parents and excuses it is a destroyer's companion; 'he that putteth his trust in the LORD shall be made fat.' Honest rebuke wins esteem. Trust in God brings true prosperity.

'He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool: but whoso walketh wisely... shall be delivered'; he that gives to the poor shall not lack, but the wicked's rise hides men away. Self-trust is folly. Generosity is rewarded; when the wicked fall, the righteous flourish.
